batch_metadata.timezone_note: Entirely EST (UTC-5). DST ended Nov 5, 2023; next transition (EDT) is Mar 10, 2024. All timestamps are local EST.

primary_flags.summary: Dec 2 — Christmas event: Melissa relays Olivia's account that Abby controlled the departure: 'Olivia is upset that Abby led you guys out of the thing last night. She said she didn't get to ice skate' (ROW-4073) + 'She said you just follow Abby around and Abby led you to the car' (ROW-4074). Eli provides corrective account: no skates available in her size (ROW-4075); sends photo proving Olivia did see Santa (ROW-4077-4078). Melissa partly acknowledges error re Santa ('Now she said she did,' ROW-4079) but does not address or correct the Abby-control attribution. Ends with 'That's terrible' (ROW-4080) — ambiguous but applied to the skate situation without challenging the Abby framing. Key elements: (1) Abby-as-controller attribution presented as fact (ADD-001); (2) Olivia's distress relayed as parental inadequacy evidence (ADD-004); (3) Partial acknowledgment of factual error without retracting the Abby framing. Continues Abby-as-obstacle arc component from B-008.

key_messages.text: Olivia is upset that Abby led you guys out of the thing last night. She said she didn't get to ice skate
She said you just follow Abby around and Abby led you to the car
They didn't have skates for her. There were people queued for them and no idea how long her size would have taken
She didn't get to see Santa either
I have a picture of her with Santa

primary_flags.summary: Dec 24 Christmas Eve — sustained cluster across logistics dispute over Eli seeing Olivia: ROW-4177 'You told her you would come then claimed you didn't remember saying that then kept giving excuses'; ROW-4178 'I tried to include you for her sake but you aren't capable of communicating or making a decision'; ROW-4184 'Since you can't grow a pair and stand up for yourself and your daughter'; ROW-4185 'You let Abby dictate everything which is why your relationship with your daughter isn't very good.' ROW-4185 is the most explicit written causal statement to date: Abby is directly cited as the reason Eli's relationship with Olivia 'isn't very good.' ROW-4184 frames the failure as Eli's inability to assert himself against Abby on Olivia's behalf. Cluster combines ADD-001 (Abby-as-controller) and ADD-002 (inadequate-father because of Abby). Second consecutive seasonal holiday trigger (Halloween B-002/B-011; Christmas Eve here). Compare to Jul 14 birthday (B-008 S001) and Dec 24 dynamics.

key_messages.text: You told her you would come then claimed you didn't remember saying that then kept giving excuses. I told you we weren't going to keep waiting on you
I tried to include you for her sake but you aren't capable of communicating or making a decision
Since you can't grow a pair and stand up for yourself and your daughter.
You let Abby dictate everything which is why your relationship with your daughter isn't very good

peripheral_flags.summary: Dec 12 — 'So if you and Abby get married my sister would have the same in hers. Daughter/sister in law Gross' — continuing the inappropriate-relationship framing of the Abby/Eli/Olivia triad; explicit 'Gross' characterization. RF-15 primary (false allegation vector); RF-01 peripheral (Abby attack vector — the characterization of the relationship as inherently inappropriate/disgusting undermines Abby's legitimacy in Olivia's life).

Jan 10: 'Or you could be a dad and do homework with her' (ROW-4313) — when Eli arranges early drop-off so Olivia can complete homework; Jan 18: 'Part of being a dad is taking care of the responsibilities too' (ROW-4343) — during homework/pickup negotiation. Both invoke inadequate-father framing (ADD-002 adjacent) in response to Eli's normal parenting logistics. RF-04/RF-01 compound peripherals; pattern of inserting 'a real dad would...' framing in routine exchanges.

Jan 20 — Third written gatekeeping admission: 'Did you try calling her?' / 'It's usually me asking her if she wants to call you' / 'I was busy and didn't do that yesterday' (ROW-4367-4369). Eli responds he tried to call her. Melissa's explanation: Olivia didn't call because Melissa forgot to prompt her. Third written confirmation of the gatekeeping mechanism first admitted in ROW-3151 (B-009 primary) and corroborated in ROW-3353-3355 (B-010 peripheral). RF-02 primary for that pass; RF-01 peripheral corroborating the phone-refusal cluster mechanism.

Jan 23 — Fourth written gatekeeping admission: 'She normally only calls because I ask if she wants to or I just dial you' / 'I didn't ask her so she didn't call' / 'Once in awhile she asks on her own' (ROW-4383-4385). Eli notes he expected Olivia to call. Melissa explains Olivia's calls are primarily Melissa-initiated. This is the clearest and most explicit of the four written admissions: 'She normally only calls because I ask if she wants to or I just dial you' — four written admissions across B-009, B-010, B-012 (x2) collectively constitute a structural documentary record of communication gatekeeping. RF-02 primary; RF-01 peripheral.

Jan 24 — 'I just told her you called and told her she should call you back but she said not right now' (ROW-4389). Melissa mediating whether Olivia returns Eli's call; Olivia declines via Melissa as intermediary. RF-02 gatekeeping peripheral; consistent with established phone-refusal mediation pattern.

ARC-RF01-001.notes: B-012 adds 2 Sev-2 signals — first multi-signal batch since B-006 (also 2 signals). ROW-4185 Christmas Eve cluster ('You let Abby dictate everything which is why your relationship with your daughter isn't very good') is a structural development: Abby-causation of Olivia relationship damage stated as explicit fact. This is the five-component arc's third component (Abby-as-obstacle/controller) reaching its most direct written expression. B-012 also adds three more gatekeeping admissions (ROW-4367-4369, ROW-4383-4385, ROW-4389) bringing the written-admission count to six total.

cp3_items.description: Abby-control attribution delivered via Olivia: 'She said you just follow Abby around and Abby led you to the car.' Eli's corrective account acknowledged only partially (Santa) while Abby framing left intact.

Christmas Eve: 'You let Abby dictate everything which is why your relationship with your daughter isn't very good.' Most explicit written causal statement: Abby directly responsible for damaged Olivia relationship. Key CP3 quote.

batch_observations.tone_assessment: Moderate conflict with significant holiday escalation. November–December period shows cooperative baseline (Christmas planning, passport coordination, sharing Olivia's activities) punctuated by two RF-01 primary clusters (Dec 2 Christmas event; Dec 24 Christmas Eve). January is largely logistics-heavy with several Eli pickup dates, Aston bullying situation (cooperative co-parenting response), and Olivia's hand injury Jan 28 (Melissa facilitates Eli's involvement, ROW-4426-4428).
batch_observations.positive_indicator_note: Two notable counter-indicators: (1) ROW-4275 — Melissa corrects Olivia's misperception that Eli was mocking her ('I told her that you weren't mocking her. You were just copying her. I told her there is a difference cause there was no ill intent') — an instance of Melissa actively defending Eli's intent to Olivia; (2) ROW-4426 — When Olivia got hurt, 'the first thing she wanted to do was call you' — Eli is still a primary attachment figure despite the ongoing alienation pattern. These are important counter-data points for the arc.
batch_observations.gatekeeping_admission_count: B-012 adds three more written gatekeeping admissions: ROW-4367-4369, ROW-4383-4385, ROW-4389. Combined with B-009 ROW-3151 and B-010 ROW-3353-3355, there are now six written admissions documenting Melissa as the primary initiator of Olivia's calls to Eli.
batch_observations.christmas_holiday_pattern: Dec 24 cluster mirrors Halloween 2022 (B-002) and Halloween 2023 (B-011 near-boundary): holiday occasions trigger Abby-prioritization framing. Eli's access on Christmas Day was ultimately limited (ROW-4192-4195: 'We're not home / She's been having too much fun at Aston's'). Christmas Day is identified as a point of limited access.
batch_observations.aston_bullying_context: ROW-4266-4281 documents collaborative parenting around Aston hitting Olivia. Both parents engaged constructively. Melissa's plan to let Olivia address it first is sound and Eli agrees. Resolution follows (ROW-4282-4287). This extended cooperative exchange is notable contrast to the Dec 24 escalation three weeks earlier.
batch_observations.passport_application: ROW-4153-4156, ROW-4195: Passport application in progress for Olivia. Eli provides birthplace information. Destination context unclear (Outer Banks is domestic; passport suggests potential international travel not previously disclosed). Monitoring for RF-02 implications.
